title: eriogonum hylophilum
intro: Eriogonum hylophilum, also known as the woodland buckwheat or woodland desert buckwheat, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States. The medicinal properties of Eriogonum hylophilum include its ability to reduce inflammation, as well as its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, as well as its role in supporting immune function. Eriogonum hylophilum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for various herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
